senses.definition: Pixels per inch; a unit describing how many picture elements fit into one inch on a display or image, indicating image sharpness.
examples: A higher ppi means a crisper image on the screen.
The monitor has 300 ppi resolution.

senses.definition: Payment Protection Insurance; a type of insurance that pays off a loan if the borrower dies, becomes seriously ill, or is unable to work.
examples: Some customers were sold PPI without proper explanations.
PPI complaints led to regulatory scrutiny.

senses.definition: PPL; shorthand for 'people' used in informal writing or texting to mean multiple persons.
examples: Lots of ppl attended the event.
Ppl online say this is the best song.

senses.definition: Push, Pull, Legs; a workout split routine in fitness training.
examples: I follow a PPL routine at the gym.
PPL days target different muscle groups.

senses.definition: Parts per million; a unit measuring small concentrations in a solution or mixture.
examples: The contaminant level is 5 ppm.
ppm is used to express trace amounts of substances.

senses.definition: Points per reception; a scoring method in some fantasy football leagues that rewards catches.
examples: This season our team uses PPR for scoring.
Players with many receptions gain more PPR points.

senses.definition: Pages per second; a measure of how many pages a printer or device outputs per second.
examples: The printer prints at 20 pps.
Higher pps means faster printing.

senses.definition: Parts per trillion; a unit denoting extremely small concentrations.
examples: This pollutant is present at 2 ppt.
ppt is used in highly sensitive measurements.

senses.definition: PowerPoint; a presentation program by Microsoft.
examples: I prepared slides in PPT for the meeting.
We used PPT to present our findings.

senses.definition: PowerPoint presentation; a slide deck created in Microsoft PowerPoint.
examples: The pptn covered quarterly results.
Please share the pptn with the team.

senses.definition: A placeholder abbreviation without a single fixed common meaning; may refer to domain-specific terms (context needed).
examples: In this document, PQ stands for project quality.
PQ metrics were discussed in the meeting.

senses.definition: PR; public relations; activities to manage how an organization is perceived.
examples: The company hired a PR firm.
PR strategy includes media outreach.

senses.definition: Praam; a specific proper name that may refer to a person, place, or brand depending on context.
examples: Praam is a village in the region.
The brand uses the name Praam for its line.

senses.definition: Pracharak; a title used in Indian religious contexts for a preacher or propagandist.
examples: The praacharaks spoke at the outreach.
Pracharak duties include teaching and guiding followers.

senses.definition: Plural of pracharak; multiple Hindu religious preachers or propagandists.
examples: The pracharaks led the annual gathering.
Pracharaks traveled to many towns.

senses.definition: An archaic or misspelled form of 'practic' not standard in modern usage; typically not used in contemporary English.
examples: This form is rarely found in modern texts.
The correct word is 'practice' or 'practicum' in many contexts.

senses.definition: Nonstandard or rare plural of 'practicability'; refers to how practicable something is; not common in everyday use.
examples: The practicabilities of this plan were debated.
We evaluated its practicabilities before proceeding.

senses.definition: The quality of being practicable; feasibility or practicality of a plan or idea.
examples: The practicability of the proposal is uncertain.
They tested the practicability of the new method.

senses.definition: Able to be done or put into practice; feasible.
examples: Many tasks are practicable with more time.
It is practicable to implement the plan now.

senses.definition: The quality of being practicable; the state of being feasible.
examples: The practicableness of the plan was debated.
We questioned the practicableness in adverse conditions.

senses.definition: Plural form of practicableness; rare in ordinary usage.
examples: They discussed the practicablenesses of several options.

senses.definition: In a practical or feasible way; in a way that can be done in practice.
examples: The plan can be implemented practicably.
They arranged the system practicably under the constraints.

senses.definition: Useful and suitable for real life; not just theoretical.
examples: They want practical solutions.
This is a practical guide to cooking.

senses.definition: A practical person; someone good at handling real-life tasks.
examples: She is a practical and reliable worker.
He relies on practical rather than theoretical knowledge.

senses.definition: A philosophy or approach that emphasizes practical results and applications over theory.
examples: The debate touched on practicalism in education.
He criticized pure theory in favor of practicalism.
